sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Newcastle United come into this match with mixed results, having won 2 and lost 2 of their last 5 games, but they’ve shown attacking intent with high expected goals and corner stats. Fulham, on the other hand, are struggling, losing 3 of their last 5 matches and conceding multiple goals in each defeat. This suggests Newcastle have the upper hand in terms of recent form.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Fulham have surprisingly dominated, winning both matches with scores of 3-1 and 2-1. Despite Newcastle often being the first to score in head-to-head games, Fulham have shown resilience to come back and secure victories.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Newcastle United rank well defensively, conceding the least goals in the league (4th) and maintaining 5 clean sheets, while also excelling in final third entries and corners (2nd). Fulham’s stats mirror Newcastle’s in some areas like goals scored (8 vs 7), but their defense has been porous, conceding 12 goals this season compared to Newcastle’s 7.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Anthony Gordon has been in fine form for Newcastle, netting 3 goals in his last 5 matches, while Bruno Guimarães has a strong record against Fulham with a high Sofascore rating. Fulham will rely on their ability to exploit late chances, as shown in goal distribution, but lack standout performers recently.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Newcastle’s superior form, home advantage at St James’ Park, and Sofascore’s 55% probability of a home win, they are favored to win. Fulham’s recent head-to-head success might give them hope, but their defensive frailties and poor away form could hinder them.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Newcastle United lead Fulham 1-0 at St James' Park. The home side secured the advantage with a well-taken goal from Jacob Murphy in the 18th minute. Despite Fulham holding a slight edge in ball possession, Newcastle have been more clinical in front of goal, capitalizing on their chances. Both teams have shown resilience in duels, with similar success rates in aerial challenges, but Newcastle's attacking efficiency has made the difference so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Early on, Fulham showed intent with some promising spells of pressure. However, Newcastle quickly shifted the momentum in their favor with sustained attacking phases around the time of their goal. After the opener, Fulham responded with multiple periods of dominance, especially in the latter stages of the half, pushing forward with intent. Towards the end, Newcastle regained some control with brief bursts of attacking play, but Fulham largely dictated the tempo as the whistle approached.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Newcastle, Jacob Murphy has been the standout player, not only scoring the decisive goal but also posing a constant threat in the final third. Bruno Guimarães and Kieran Trippier have also been influential, contributing with key passes and defensive stability. On Fulham's side, players like Saša Lukić and Sander Berge have tried to drive their team forward with creative play, while Raúl Jiménez remains a potential danger up top despite not finding the net yet. Fulham will need their attacking players to convert their entries into the final third into clear chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Newcastle have adopted a balanced approach, focusing on quick transitions and exploiting spaces in Fulham's defense, as evidenced by their goal. Their ability to win possession in the final third has been crucial. Fulham, on the other hand, have controlled more of the ball and made frequent entries into dangerous areas but have lacked the finishing touch. Their higher number of final third entries suggests they can create opportunities, but they need to be more decisive in the penalty area. Defensively, both teams have held firm, though Newcastle's backline, led by players like Trippier, has been more effective at neutralizing threats.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Fulham push harder for an equalizer, given their possession dominance and frequent forays into Newcastle's half. They will need to improve their shot accuracy and capitalize on set pieces or counterattacks. Newcastle, buoyed by their lead and home support, will probably look to manage the game while seeking opportunities to extend their advantage through quick breaks. Players like Anthony Gordon and Murphy could be key for Newcastle, while Fulham will rely on Jiménez to lead their attack. Expect a competitive half with both teams having chances to alter the scoreline.
